dna.divergenceRationale: All three recent builds used either centered or split-image heroes with warm-editorial or centered-classic archetypes — this build moves into the editorial/bold family with an asymmetric-left hero that anchors Dr. Cortez's specialist credentials in a high-contrast navy field, deliberately refusing the centered warmth of spring-st-dentistry and the split-image softness of magic-fox-orthodontics. The editorial-asymmetric archetype earns the dual-specialty, board-certified positioning that warm-editorial would undercut — a pediatric specialist with USC/UCLA affiliations should feel more like a children's hospital authority than a neighborhood family clinic.
dna.creativeDirection: Navy authority offset by a single teal gesture — the site reads like a specialist's letterhead brought to life: structured, credentialed, and precise, with just enough warmth in the typography and accent touches to signal that Dr. Cortez understands small patients and anxious parents, not just clinical outcomes.
dna.writingTone: Warm, clear, specific to Long Beach.
dna.brandSummary: Pediatric Dental Specialists, led by Dr. Cortez, serving Long Beach, — Navy authority offset by a single teal gesture — the site reads like a specialist's letterhead brought to life: structured, credentialed, and precise, with just enough warmth in the typography and accent touches to signal that Dr. Cortez understands small patients and anxious parents, not just clinical outcomes.
doRules: Use the archetype's established editorial-asymmetric layout — don't mix incompatible grid styles mid-page.
Hero variant must be asymmetric-left — lock this choice.
Card treatment is ghost — apply consistently across all card instances.
Phone number must be a tel: link in the mobile header, minimum 44px touch target.
Doctor name and credentials must appear without scrolling on the homepage.
Primary CTA copy must reference a specific action and ideally the doctor or city.
dontRules: No gradient text (background-clip: text) — AI-slop tell #1.
No faux-glass / backdrop-filter cards unless archetype explicitly calls for it.
No centered-headline + two-CTA + microlinks hero — the most overused AI pattern.
No stat-bar section if yearsExperience/happyPatients data is missing — render empty = fail.
No stock photography of smiling model families.

No generic, boilerplate, or template-sounding copy. Every headline, tagline, and CTA must reference this specific practice, doctor, or city — never industry-generic phrasing that could appear on any competitor site.
No archetype+hero combo already used in recent own-builds.
No section that renders with dashes, nulls, or placeholder text visible to users.
dna.evalRationale: Candidate 2 earns the win by pairing the editorial-asymmetric archetype with ghost card treatment — a combination that creates the most visually distinct result from the recent own-builds while also being internally the most cohesive: ghost cards, sm radius, and subtle motion all reinforce the 'specialist's letterhead' precision of the creative direction without the softness that bordered-flat or soft-shadow would reintroduce. Candidates 0 and 1 share identical archetype, hero variant, and motion, differing only in card treatment, making them insufficiently differentiated from each other and only marginally divergent from the prior warm-editorial builds.
